rationale: As a comparison source-native attempt, gazelle_cc can generate fine-grained cc_library targets for a coherent subtree like common/; scoped narrowly since the full tree relies on CMake-generated headers (build-info.h) and configure defines that Gazelle cannot synthesize.
module_bazel:
module(
    name = "llama_cpp",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_cc", version = "0.2.19")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle_cc", version = "0.6.0")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")
bazel_dep(name = "platforms", version = "1.1.0")
bazel_dep(name = "bazel_skylib", version = "1.9.0")

cc_configure = use_extension("@rules_cc//cc:extensions.bzl", "cc_configure_extension")
use_repo(cc_configure, "local_config_cc")
root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")
load("@gazelle_cc//:def.bzl", "GAZELLE_CC_LANGUAGES")

# gazelle:exclude examples
# gazelle:exclude conversion
# gazelle:exclude vendor
# gazelle:exclude ggml
# gazelle:exclude tests
# gazelle:exclude tools
# gazelle:exclude src

gazelle(
    name = "gazelle",
    gazelle = "@gazelle_cc//:gazelle_cc",
)
anticipated_risks: heavy interdependence on ggml vendor headers
missing include paths Gazelle can't infer
